name: Cresta del Gallo and El Valle Park Loop
name_original: El Garruchal, La Naveta, Cresta del Gallo – Ruta Circular – El Valle y Carrascoy
description: Explore the El Garruchal, La Naveta, Cresta del Gallo mountain bike route, a moderate 25.3-mile loop with stunning views over Murcia.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Rio Segura offers diverse terrain, from riverine paths along the Segura River to challenging mountainous regions like Sierra de Segura and Sierra de Espuña. The landscape features a mix of forest tracks, unpaved paths, and some paved sections, providing varied experiences for riders. This region is characterized by its central river, which carves through varied terrain, and surrounding areas with significant elevation changes and geological features. The trails cater to a range of skill levels, from easy routes to demanding segments. Best mountain bike trails around Rio Segura The most popular mountain bike trail is Coto Cuadros and Santomera Reservoir Loop, a 21.3 miles (34.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Coto Cuadros area and offers views of the Santomera Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cresta del Gallo and El Valle Park Loop, a moderate 25.3 miles (40.7 km) path. This route traverses the scenic Cresta del Gallo and the natural park of El Valle. Local mountain bikers also love the Ermita San Antonio El Pobre, Pico del Relojero, Minotauro – Ruta Circular – El Valle y Carrascoy, a 21.8 miles (35.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and natural parkland, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Rio Segura is defined by riverine landscapes, mountainous regions, and diverse forest tracks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy greenways to challenging climbs. The routes in Rio Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 700 reviews. More than 8000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Rio Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Rio Segura region, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: The Rio Segura region offers a wide variety of mountain bike trails, with over 420 routes available. These cater to all skill levels, including approximately 60 easy routes, 247 moderate routes, and 120 challenging trails for experienced riders.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see while mountain biking around Rio Segura?
A: Mountain biking in Rio Segura offers diverse scenery, from the riverine landscapes of the Segura River to mountainous regions like Sierra de Segura and Sierra de Espuña. You can encounter historical sites such as the 15th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope&lt;/a&gt;, the historic La Contraparada dam, and the La Ñora Water Wheel. Trails also feature natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1562562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Relojero Peak&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, along with various viewpoints offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families in Rio Segura?
A: Yes, the Rio Segura region has numerous easy routes perfect for families, including greenways with flat terrain and agricultural views. These trails often follow the river, providing a pleasant and accessible cycling experience for all ages.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in the Rio Segura area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the region offers over 120 difficult routes. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e920493212/ermita-san-antonio-el-pobre-pico-del-relojero-minotauro-ruta-circular-el-valle-y-carrascoy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita San Antonio El Pobre, Pico del Relojero, Minotauro – Ruta Circular – El Valle y Carrascoy&lt;/a&gt; or the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e936473226/sierra-de-ricote-y-la-navela-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Ricote y La Navela Loop&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation changes and varied terrain, including forest tracks and unpaved paths, testing both fitness and technical skills.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Rio Segura?
A: The mountain bike trails in Rio Segura are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, ranging from scenic riverine paths to challenging mountainous sections, and the well-maintained network of forest tracks and unpaved paths.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Rio Segura region?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Rio Segura are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e921195130/coto-cuadros-and-santomera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto Cuadros and Santomera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927716334/cresta-del-gallo-and-el-valle-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cresta del Gallo and El Valle Park Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied experiences without needing a return trip.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Rio Segura?
A: Many trails in the Rio Segura region are suitable for dogs, especially those in natural parks and less populated areas. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas, as some may have restrictions or require dogs to be on a leash. Ensure your dog is fit for the distance and terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Rio Segura?
A: The Rio Segura region generally offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Summers can be hot, especially inland, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable. Winter can bring cooler temperatures and occasional snow or mud in higher mountainous areas like Sierra de Segura, adding to the challenge for experienced riders.

Q: Are there any routes that feature water bodies like rivers or reservoirs?
A: Many routes in the Rio Segura region incorporate the river and its associated features. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e921195130/coto-cuadros-and-santomera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto Cuadros and Santomera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Santomera Reservoir. Other trails follow the Segura River itself, providing scenic riverine paths and opportunities to see historical water infrastructure like the La Contraparada dam and the La Ñora Water Wheel.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by historic sites or cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, the riverbanks and surrounding areas are rich in history. Routes often pass by sites such as the historic La Contraparada dam, the 15th-century La Ñora Water Wheel, and the Contraparada Park Bridge. You might also encounter watchtowers like the Coto Cuadros Watchtower, offering both historical interest and scenic views of the landscape.

Q: Are there options for parking or public transport access near the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific parking and public transport options vary by trailhead, many routes in the Rio Segura region are accessible from towns and villages that offer parking facilities. For routes closer to urban centers, public transport might be an option, but it's best to research specific starting points for detailed access information.
tips: This promenade linked to the main events of the capital dates back to the 15th century, although it was rebuilt in the 18th century. It is one of the most emblematic meeting places in the city.
Paseo Murcia River 2 km. on the banks of the Segura River.

This is a very busy route for cyclists, especially on weekends. Good route to take if you want to get to the coast. El Garruchal is one of the three natural ports that exist in the chain of mountains that close the Segura river valley to the south, separating it from the coastal depression of the Mar Menor.

This paved port is famous because it has been included in the circuit of the Cycling Tour of Spain on several occasions. It is demanding, but worth the effort. Once at the summit, all that remains is to enjoy the splendid landscape.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the El Garruchal, La Naveta, Cresta del Gallo mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route, covering over 40 km, typically takes around 3 hours and 16 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as 'moderate' and requires good fitness. While some sections are demanding, particularly the climb to Cresta del Gallo with gradients up to 14-15%, the overall route is often described as suitable for all skill levels. Beginners with good stamina might find it challenging but rewarding.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll encounter paved sections, especially on climbs like Cresta del Gallo, which has been part of the Cycling Tour of Spain. However, some segments are unpaved, featuring rough asphalt, gravel trails, and singletrack sections with steep rock gardens and pedaling challenges within the wider El Valle y Carrascoy park.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and viewpoints can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to splendid landscapes, particularly from the summit of Cresta del Gallo, offering extensive views over the city of Murcia and the surrounding hinterland. The route blends urban and natural vistas, passing through the 'green lung' of Murcia, the Parque Regional de El Valle y Carrascoy, with its woodlands and scrubland. On clear days, you might even spot the Mar Menor from the peak of La Panocha.

faqs.question: Are there any specific highlights or landmarks to look out for?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes several notable points. You'll go by the Malecón Botanical Garden and the Murcia Río Riverside Walk. The climb takes you over Garruchal Pass and past the distinctive 'rooster's comb' rock formations of Cresta del Gallo, including 'La Panocha'. The Base of the Cresta del Gallo is also a key point.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, so you can start from various points. For specific parking information, it's best to check local maps or community forums for recommended starting points near El Garruchal or within the El Valle y Carrascoy area, as dedicated trailhead parking might not be explicitly marked for this specific loop.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Murcia region can experience high temperatures, which can affect the road surface on climbs like Cresta del Gallo. Spring and autumn generally offer more moderate temperatures, making them ideal for mountain biking. Early mornings are also recommended during warmer months.

faqs.question: Is the El Garruchal, La Naveta, Cresta del Gallo mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The route is located within the Parque Regional de El Valle y Carrascoy. Generally, dogs are allowed in regional parks in Spain, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always check specific park regulations before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in Parque Regional de El Valle y Carrascoy?
faqs.answer: As a regional park, there are typically no entrance fees to access the natural areas for activities like mountain biking. However, always respect park regulations regarding trail usage, environmental protection, and any specific rules that might apply to certain zones or activities within the park.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other mountain biking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Itinerario 15 Murcia - Torre Pacheco, Ruta Ciclosegura - Itinerario 2, Río Segura, and Transmurciana 01. Other intersecting paths include Antonio Pérez Nortes, Cresta del Gallo por La Tana, and the Vía Verde de la Costera Sur, among many others.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or strategic tips for tackling the Cresta del Gallo climb?
faqs.answer: The climb to Cresta del Gallo is a significant challenge, featuring an average gradient of 6.5% with steeper ramps up to 14-15%. The road surface can be rough asphalt with occasional pits. Pace yourself on the ascent, especially if temperatures are high. Once at the summit, the terrain can become very flat, offering a welcome respite and rewarding views.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Parque Regional de El Valle y Carrascoy is a protected natural area with rich biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, including potentially golden eagles. Wild boars are also known to inhabit the park. Keep an eye out for the diverse Mediterranean flora, with over 600 plant species present.

waypoints.name: Base of the Cresta del Gallo
